name: Crew Arrival at Kennedy
description: The Artemis II crew arrives at Kennedy Space Center and answers questions from credentialed media.

name: Prelaunch News Conference
description: NASA holds a prelaunch news conference on countdown status with launch team representatives and weather officers.

name: Tanking Operations Begin
description: Coverage of tanking operations to load propellant into the SLS rocket begins, including views of the rocket.

name: Artemis II Launch
description: Targeted launch of the Artemis II mission from Launch Complex 39B at Kennedy Space Center.

name: Translunar Injection Burn
description: Planned translunar injection burn for five minutes and 49 seconds to send the crew in Orion toward the Moon.

name: Live Crew Downlink
description: First live video downlink event from the Orion spacecraft as it travels toward the Moon.

name: Lunar Imaging Operations
description: The crew conducts lunar imaging for flyby operations.

name: Outbound Trajectory Correction Burn
description: Orion performs its third outbound trajectory correction burn to fine-tune its path to the Moon.

name: Enter Lunar Sphere of Influence
description: The Orion spacecraft officially enters the Moon's sphere of influence.

name: Apollo 13 Distance Record Broken
description: The crew surpasses the record for human's farthest distance from Earth previously set by Apollo 13, at 248,655 miles.

name: Loss of Communications (Blackout)
description: Predicted loss of communications as the crew heads behind the far side of the Moon (estimated 40-min).

name: Lunar Closest Approach
description: Orion reaches its closest approach to the Moon during the flyby.

name: Maximum Distance from Earth
description: Orion reaches its maximum distance from Earth during the mission.

name: Solar Eclipse Period
description: Orion enters a period with the Moon eclipsing the Sun.

name: Call with ISS Astronauts
description: The Artemis II crew speaks with the astronauts aboard the International Space Station in an audio-only conversation.

name: Return Trajectory Correction Burn
description: Orion performs its first return trajectory correction burn.

name: In-Orbit Crew News Conference
description: The crew holds a live news conference from orbit.

name: Crew & Service Module Separation
description: The Orion crew module separates from the European Service Module ahead of re-entry.

name: Splashdown
description: Splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. Recovery teams assist the crew out of Orion.

name: Post-Splashdown News Conference
description: Post-splashdown news conference at NASA Johnson Space Center.
